title: Drip vs Buttondown (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Drip vs Buttondown: compare pricing, features, and real use cases to find the right email tool for your needs.
intro: Choosing between Drip and Buttondown comes down to your specific use case. Both are legitimate email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. Drip is built for e-commerce businesses wanting deep store integration, while Buttondown targets developers and technical writers who prefer markdown. This comparison covers the key differences so you can decide quickly.

comparison_table.tool_a: E-Commerce Businesses Wanting Deep Store Integration
comparison_table.tool_b: Developers And Technical Writers Who Prefer Markdown

choose_a_if: You are primarily focused on e-commerce businesses wanting deep store integration
choose_b_if: You are primarily focused on developers and technical writers who prefer markdown

faq.a: It depends on your use case. Drip wins for e-commerce businesses wanting deep store integration, while Buttondown is stronger for developers and technical writers who prefer markdown. Neither is objectively better — the right choice depends on your specific needs.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Drip or Buttondown?
faq.a: Buttondown starts at a lower price point ($9/mo vs $39/mo). However, pricing scales with subscriber count, so the total cost depends on your list size.

faq.q: Can I switch from Buttondown to Drip?
faq.a: Yes, switching from Buttondown to Drip is possible. Most platforms support CSV import/export for subscriber lists. Plan for some time to recreate automations and templates, as these rarely transfer directly between platforms.
